sections.paragraph:
ρέζω

I. to do, act, deal, Od.:—absol., Hom.:—c. acc. rei, to do, accomplish, make, Hom., etc.: —Pass., μῆχος ρεχθέντος κακοῦ a remedy for mischief once done, Il.

2. c. dupl. acc. pers. et rei, to do something to one, κακὸν ρέζειν τινά Hom.; ἀγαθὰ ρ. τινά Hom.; also more rarely c. dat. pers., μηκέτι μοι κακὰ ρέζετε do me no more mischiefs, Od.; ὅσα βροτοῖς ἔρεξας κακά Eur.

3. with strengthd. signf., εἴ τι ρέξει if it shall avail aught, be of any service, Il.

II. in spec. sense, to perform sacrifices, Hom., Soph.; absol. to do sacrifice, Lat. operari, facere, ρέζειν θεῶι Hom.:—sometimes with the victim in acc., ρέξω βοῦν ἦνιν will sacrifice it, Soph.
